subquestions.question_text: Which sketch best represents egg weights normally distributed with mean \(48.8\text{ g}\) and standard deviation \(3.40\text{ g}\)?

options.label: A
options.text: A right-skewed curve centred at \(48.8\text{ g}\).

options.label: C
options.text: A symmetric bell curve centred at \(48.8\text{ g}\).

options.label: D
options.text: A symmetric bell curve centred at \(3.40\text{ g}\).

subquestions.question_text: Find the maximum weight of a small egg, where small means the lightest \(25\%\).
Find the minimum weight of a big egg, where big means heavier than \(75\%\) of eggs.
In a sample of 50 eggs, estimate the number weighing less than \(46\text{ g}\).
In a sample of 50 eggs, estimate the number weighing between \(45\text{ g}\) and \(55\text{ g}\).
Two people are chosen at random. Find the probability that both have savings between \(\$2800\) and \(\$5200\).
Find the amount needed to be in the top \(10\%\) of wealth.
If \(75\%\) of people have less than \(d\) dollars, find \(d\).
Find the proportion of people with less than \(\$3000\).
Find the probability that all 10 alarms work properly.
Find the probability that at least 8 of the 10 alarms work properly.
Find the probability that the alarm rings when the squirrel runs in front of one house.
Find the probability that the squirrel makes at least 3 alarm systems ring.
Find the probability that a watermelon has diameter less than \(10\text{ cm}\).
Find the probability that a watermelon has diameter more than \(15\text{ cm}\).
If \(20\%\) of watermelons have diameter bigger than \(d\), find \(d\) to the nearest integer.
Find the probability that a randomly selected watermelon is discarded because its diameter is less than \(8.5\text{ cm}\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected car is expensive, where expensive means priced between \(\$40000\) and \(\$48000\).
For a central probability of \(0.6\), find \(q\) where the interval is symmetric about \(\$25000\).
Find the probability that a bag of potatoes weighs less than \(700\text{ g}\).
In a random selection of 20 bags, find the probability that exactly 1 weighs less than \(700\text{ g}\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected hamburger weighs exactly \(210\text{ g}\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected hamburger weighs more than \(240\text{ g}\).
For 10 hamburgers, find the probability that fewer than 4 weigh more than \(240\text{ g}\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected burger weighs more than \(240\text{ g}\).
Given that a randomly selected burger weighs more than \(240\text{ g}\), find the probability that it is a cheeseburger.
Find the probability that a randomly selected flour pack is rejected.
Estimate the number of rejected packs in a sample of 100.
Given that a bag is not rejected, find the probability that it has mass greater than \(502\text{ g}\).
Which sketch represents grades normally distributed with mean 6 and standard deviation 1, including 1 and 2 standard deviations above the mean?

options.label: A
options.text: A bell curve centred at 6 with marks at 7 and 8.

options.label: B
options.text: A bell curve centred at 1 with marks at 6 and 8.

options.label: D
options.text: A bell curve centred at 6 with marks at 5 and 4 only.

subquestions.question_text: How many standard deviations is a grade of 5.5 away from the mean?
Find the probability that a student gets a grade higher than \(7.5\).
Find the probability that a randomly chosen student scores between 6 and 8.
Out of 30 students, estimate how many score between 6 and 8.
If 75% of students scored less than \(X\), find \(X\).
Which sketch correctly represents parcel masses with mean \(24\text{ kg}\) and standard deviation \(5\text{ kg}\)?

options.label: A
options.text: A bell curve centred at \(5\), with marks at \(24\) and \(34\).

options.label: B
options.text: A bell curve centred at \(24\), with marks at \(19,29,14,34\).

options.label: D
options.text: A bell curve centred at \(29\), with marks at \(24\) and \(34\).

subquestions.question_text: State how many standard deviations \(34\text{ kg}\) is above the mean.
A depot processes 240 parcels. Estimate how many have masses between \(22\text{ kg}\) and \(30\text{ kg}\).
If 85% of parcels have mass less than \(k\), find \(k\).
Find \(a\) and \(b\) for the central region representing probability \(0.6826\).
Estimate the mean mass of the oranges from the grouped table.
Estimate the standard deviation of the orange masses.
Estimate the percentage of oranges with mass greater than \(165\text{ g}\).
If 8% of oranges are very small with mass less than \(y\), find \(y\).
Estimate the percentage of oranges with masses between \(131\text{ g}\) and \(165\text{ g}\).
The shipment contains approximately 18000 oranges. Estimate how many are heavy.
Find the probability that the featured book on a given day is a mystery novel.
Over 30 days, find the probability that a mystery novel is chosen exactly 9 times.
Over 30 days, find the probability that a non-mystery book is chosen at least 18 times.
Find the probability that a battery lasts between 40 and 45 hours.
If 85% of batteries last less than \(m\) hours, find \(m\).
Find \(a\) and \(b\) such that \(P(a<T<b)=0.95\) and the interval is symmetric about 42.
Find the probability that exactly 4 customers choose the limited-edition cupcake.
Find the probability that at least 3 customers choose the limited-edition cupcake.
Find the probability that at least 3 customers from the first branch and at least 6 from the second branch choose the cupcake.
For jar volume \(V\sim N(510,8^2)\), state \(P(V<510)\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected jar is rejected if rejected means volume less than \(498\text{ ml}\).
For 12 selected jars, find the probability exactly 2 are rejected.
Find the minimum volume required for a jar to be labelled large capacity, the highest 15%.
Find the probability that a box of 12 jars contains at least 10 jars that are not rejected.
Sophia speaks to 10 customers and sells warranties with probability 0.35. Find the probability she sells at least one warranty.
Find the probability Sophia sells exactly 4 warranties.
A fair die is rolled 15 times and a point is scored each time a 6 appears. Find the expected number of points.
Find the probability that the player scores exactly 3 points.
Find the probability that the player scores more than 3 points.
Write down the distribution used to model the number of successful serves.
Which assumption is needed for this binomial model?

options.label: A
options.text: Each serve is independent with the same probability of success.

options.label: B
options.text: The probability of success changes after each serve.

options.label: C
options.text: The serves follow a normal distribution.

subquestions.question_text: Calculate the probability of exactly 13 successful serves.
Calculate the probability of at least 12 successful serves.
Find \(E(X)\).
Find \(\operatorname{Var}(X)\).
Find the probability that a carton contains less than \(990\text{ ml}\).
Find the probability that a carton contains more than \(1012\text{ ml}\).
In a sample of 600 cartons, estimate the number with volumes between \(990\text{ ml}\) and \(1000\text{ ml}\).
In a sample of 600 cartons, estimate the number with volumes between \(1000\text{ ml}\) and \(1012\text{ ml}\).
State the null and alternative hypotheses for testing whether carton volumes follow the stated normal distribution.

options.label: A
options.text: \(H_0:\) volumes do not follow \(N(1000,6^2)\); \(H_1:\) they do.

options.label: C
options.text: \(H_0:\) all classes have equal frequency; \(H_1:\) they do not.

options.label: D
options.text: \(H_0:\) volumes follow \(N(1000,6^2)\); \(H_1:\) volumes do not follow this distribution.

subquestions.question_text: Find the p-value of the goodness-of-fit test.
State the conclusion at the 5% significance level.

options.label: D
options.text: Accept \(H_0\), since the model is exact.
